danny-avila/LibreChat#5187.body:
Hi,
instead of creating micro-issues, I believe it's better to sum up my accessibility findings from a screenreader user's perspective here centrally.  
* In the presets view, hover elements are used to show buttons to PIN or remove a preset, They're not keyboard-focusable. Those buttons should get labelled and focusable.  
* Prompts: In the prompt management, there are a lot of unlabelled buttons. I cannot tell for each one what it's supposed to do, but examples are buttons to rename or delete.  
* In the prompt createion field, every text box is labelled, there's however an unlabelled checkbox which I don't know what it's meant for.  
* In teh agent builder, after choosing model parameters, the button to save changes and get back to the builder is unlabelled. Same applies for the button to choose an avatar, or to get back from tools/agents view  
* When you add a tool to an agent that requires to enter an API key, the input box to open that key should automatically get focus. Otherwise, it just appears on top of the tools list, and I as the user wonder why nothing happens when I press the add button.  
* When pasting images into the chat, two unlabelled buttons appear: One to delete the image, and one that has the collapsed attribute - but nothing expands when pressed.  
* Online on the Code Interpreter website, the button to copy a created API key is unlabelled.
Thank you very much for your work on making LibreChat more accessible. As an idea, I could imagine that integrating automatic test tools into the Pipeline such as Lighthouse CI could help to find such things faster, but that's just an idea of mine.
danny-avila/LibreChat#5187.title: Accessibility issues for screenreader users

danny-avila/LibreChat#6710.body:
### What features would you like to see added?

Currently, AWS bedrock allows you to [create custom inference profiles](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/bedrock/latest/userguide/inference-profiles-create.html) for models. For example:

```
aws bedrock create-inference-profile --inference-profile-name "myappinferenceprofile" --model-source "copyFrom=arn:aws:bedrock:us-west-2:123456789123:inference-profile/us.anthropic.claude-3-7-sonnet-20250219-v1:0"
```

```
aws bedrock list-inference-profiles --type-equals "APPLICATION"

--This will list all application inference profiles showing the ARN, profile name and ID for each. Just an example, model names are different from real for Anthropic:

{
    "inferenceProfileName": "ch-map-us-anthropic-claude-3-7-sonnet-20250219-v1-0",
    "createdAt": "2025-04-01T12:00:46.784352+00:00",
    "updatedAt": "2025-04-01T12:00:46.784352+00:00",
    "inferenceProfileArn": "arn:aws:bedrock:us-east-1:123456789123:application-inference-profile/rf3zeruqfake",
    "models": [
        {
            "modelArn": "arn:aws:bedrock:us-east-1::foundation-model/anthropic.claude-3-7-sonnet-20250219-v1:0"
        },
        {
            "modelArn": "arn:aws:bedrock:us-east-2::foundation-model/anthropic.claude-3-7-sonnet-20250219-v1:0"
        },
        {
            "modelArn": "arn:aws:bedrock:us-west-2::foundation-model/anthropic.claude-3-7-sonnet-20250219-v1:0"
        }
    ],
    "inferenceProfileId": "rf3zeruqfake",
    "status": "ACTIVE",
    "type": "APPLICATION"
}
```

Once the profile is created, to use it in LibreChat you need to add the new profile arn to the list of models:
```
BEDROCK_AWS_MODELS=arn:aws:bedrock:us-east-1:123456789123:application-inference-profile/rf3zeruqfake
```
But Librechat performs a check against model name:
```
if (/claude-3[-.]7/.test(model)) {...
```
and enables different parameters (thinking, temperature, topP and topK, etc) based on this check. You cannot control ARN name, and you cannot use profile name instead. All in all, this leads to a model that you cannot use in agents because of no settings are available:

![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/405f145f-856c-469f-b2dc-7b24f9b45da1)


### More details

Provided above

Endpoints

### Pictures

danny-avila/LibreChat#7109.body:
### What features would you like to see added?

Amazon Bedrock has made a **prompt caching** feature publicly available see [here](https://aws.amazon.com/blogs/machine-learning/effectively-use-prompt-caching-on-amazon-bedrock/).


### More details

The prompt caching works by adding a cache marker to a part of the prompt that remains static. In case of LibreChat, I believe that this would be beneficial for:

- system prompts - namely system prompts for agents with many tools can get quite long and remain static over each message
- documents added as text - if a user adds documents as text to conversation, this remains static over the conversation
- tool call results (?)

The prompt caching can lead to **decrease of API usage costs** as cache read input tokens are 10 times cheaper that regular input tokens.

danny-avila/LibreChat#8444.body:
# Model Grouping Implementation Issues

## Date: 2025-07-14

## Problem Statement
Need to organize models in LibreChat by developer/provider groups (OpenAI, Anthropic, Google, etc.) while maintaining all requests through OpenRouter. The original configuration had a flat list of models with rich descriptions and pricing information.

## Original Configuration (Working)
- Used `modelSpecs` with detailed model information
- Each model had:
  - Clean labels (e.g., "GPT-4o", "Claude 4 Sonnet")
  - Pricing indicators ($, $$, $$$, etc.)
  - Detailed descriptions of capabilities
  - Custom settings (temperature, max tokens, etc.)
  - Special features (Pi's greeting, prompt prefixes)

## Goals (ALL Required)
1. **Group models by developer** - Create expandable categories (OpenAI, Anthropic, Google, etc.)
2. **Preserve model descriptions** - Keep pricing indicators ($, $$, $$$) and capability descriptions
3. **Maintain clean labels** - Show "GPT-4o" not "openai/gpt-4o"
4. **Route through OpenRouter** - All requests must go through OpenRouter API
5. **No code changes** - Solution must be configuration-only to avoid merge conflicts

## Attempt 1: Custom Endpoints for Grouping ❌ FAILED TO MEET ALL GOALS

### Implementation
Created multiple custom endpoints, each representing a developer group:
```yaml
endpoints:
  custom:
    - name: "OpenAI Models"
      apiKey: "${OPENROUTER_KEY}"
      baseURL: "https://openrouter.ai/api/v1"
      iconURL: "openAI"
      models:
        default: ["openai/gpt-4o", "openai/o3"]
      # ... etc for each group
```

### Goals Achieved vs Failed
- ✅ Goal 1: Group models by developer - YES, created expandable groups
- ❌ Goal 2: Preserve model descriptions - NO, lost ALL descriptions and pricing
- ❌ Goal 3: Maintain clean labels - NO, shows raw API names "openai/gpt-4o"
- ✅ Goal 4: Route through OpenRouter - YES, maintained
- ✅ Goal 5: No code changes - YES, configuration only

### Critical Failures
- **"AGENTS" appeared as top model option** - Unexpected, not in our configuration
- **No descriptions field** in custom endpoint models
- **No label customization** - always shows raw model ID
- **No pricing indicators** - users can't see cost differences
- **No capability descriptions** - users don't know what each model is for

## Attempt 2: Hybrid Approach (ModelSpecs + Custom Endpoints) ❌ COMPLETE FAILURE

### Implementation
Tried to use both systems together:
```yaml
interface:
  modelSelect: true

modelSpecs:
  enforce: false  # Changed from true
  prioritize: true
  addedEndpoints:
    - "OpenAI Models"
    - "Anthropic Models"
    - "Google Models"
    - "Open Source Models"
    - "Other Models"
  list:
    # All original model specs with descriptions
```

### Goals Achieved vs Failed
- ❓ Goal 1: Group models by developer - UNKNOWN, UI broke
- ❓ Goal 2: Preserve model descriptions - UNKNOWN, couldn't test properly
- ❓ Goal 3: Maintain clean labels - UNKNOWN, UI malfunction
- ✅ Goal 4: Route through OpenRouter - YES in theory
- ✅ Goal 5: No code changes - YES, configuration only

### Critical Failures
- **UI didn't work as expected** - Model selector didn't properly merge the two systems
- **addedEndpoints misunderstanding** - Feature designed for standard endpoints (openAI, google, etc.), NOT custom endpoints
- **Incompatible systems** - modelSpecs and custom endpoints don't integrate well

## Technical Limitations Discovered

1. **Custom Endpoints Limitations**
   - Cannot add descriptions to individual models
   - Cannot customize display names (always shows raw model ID)
   - No support for pricing indicators
   - No per-model metadata

2. **ModelSpecs Limitations**
   - No built-in grouping/categorization feature
   - `addedEndpoints` doesn't work with custom endpoints as expected
   - Setting `enforce: false` creates unexpected UI behavior

3. **UI Constraints**
   - The model selector is designed for either modelSpecs OR endpoints, not both
   - No native support for hierarchical model organization

## Current State
**REVERTED TO ORIGINAL** - No grouping, but all descriptions preserved.

## Summary of Failures

### What We Wanted (All 5 Goals)
1. Models grouped by developer (OpenAI, Anthropic, etc.)
2. Keep descriptions and pricing ($, $$, $$$)
3. Clean model names ("GPT-4o" not "openai/gpt-4o")
4. Route everything through OpenRouter
5. Configuration-only solution

### What We Got
- **Attempt 1**: Grouping worked BUT lost all descriptions, pricing, and clean names (2/5 goals)
- **Attempt 2**: Complete failure, UI broke, "AGENTS" appeared mysteriously (0/5 goals testable)

### What We Lost When Using Grouping
1. **Descriptive Labels**: "GPT-4o" → "openai/gpt-4o"
2. **Pricing Info**: "$$ - OpenAI's flagship model..." → (none)
3. **Capabilities Descriptions**: Detailed explanations → (none)
4. **Special Features**: Pi's greeting, custom prompts → (none)
5. **User Guidance**: Which model to use for what → (none)

## Ideal Solution (Would Require Code Changes)
Add a `group` field to modelSpecs:
```yaml
modelSpecs:
  list:
    - name: "gpt-4o-clean"
      label: "GPT-4o"
      group: "OpenAI Models"  # NEW FIELD
      description: "$$ - OpenAI's flagship model..."
```

## Conclusion

**BOTH ATTEMPTS FAILED** to achieve all required goals simultaneously.

LibreChat's configuration system forces an impossible choice:
- **EITHER** have model grouping (lose descriptions, pricing, clean names)
- **OR** have rich model information (lose grouping)

The system is fundamentally unable to support both organizational hierarchy AND descriptive metadata through configuration alone. This is a significant limitation that degrades user experience no matter which approach is chosen.

### Final Decision
Reverted to original configuration (no grouping) because:
- Descriptions and pricing are MORE important than grouping
- Users need to know what models cost and what they're good for
- Clean model names are essential for usability
- A flat list with good descriptions > grouped list with no context

danny-avila/LibreChat#8921.body: ### What happened? **Issue Description:** The frequency penalty slider in the model settings is limited to a range of 0 to 2, but the tooltip documentation correctly states the range should be "Number between -2.0 and 2.0" according to the OpenAI API specification. **Expected Behavior:** The frequency penalty slider should allow the full range from -2.0 to 2.0, matching both the OpenAI API specification and the tooltip documentation. **Actual Behavior:** The slider only allows values from 0 to 2, preventing users from setting negative frequency penalty values that can be useful for encouraging repetition. **Root Cause:** The issue was in the backend schema configuration where `openAISettings` and `agentsSettings` incorrectly defined the minimum value as `0` instead of `-2` for both `frequency_penalty` and `presence_penalty` settings. ### Version Information ghcr.io/danny-avila/librechat-dev latest 12dbb6d8404d 12 minutes ago 1.24GB ghcr.io/danny-avila/librechat-rag-api-dev-lite latest 8858d2bd9ff5 4 weeks ago 1.48GB ### Steps to Reproduce 1. Open LibreChat in a web browser 2. Start a new conversation with any OpenAI model (GPT-4, GPT-3.5, etc.) 3. Click on the settings/parameters panel 4. Locate the "Frequency Penalty" slider 5. Try to drag the slider to the left (negative values) 6. Observe that the slider cannot go below 0 7. Hover over the frequency penalty label to see the tooltip 8. Note the inconsistency: tooltip says "-2.0 and 2.0" but slider only goes "0 to 2" ### What browsers are you seeing the problem on? Chrome ### Relevant log output ```shell No error logs are generated for this issue as it's a configuration problem rather than a runtime error. The issue is visible in the UI behavior and can be confirmed by inspecting the schema configuration. **File affected:** `packages/data-provider/src/schemas.ts` **Lines:** OpenAI settings (lines ~210-221) and Agent settings (lines ~376-387) ``` ### Screenshots ### Code of Conduct - [x] I agree to follow this project's Code of Conduct

danny-avila/LibreChat#9013.body:
### What happened?

When deleting a bookmark, an error toast appears: “There was an error deleting the bookmark.” The bookmark is actually deleted successfully, and no server errors are logged. Expected behavior: only the success toast should display.

Root cause: the cache updater for tag deletion attempted to deep-clone a possibly undefined allConversations infinite query via JSON.parse(JSON.stringify(data)), which can throw and trigger the error toast despite successful deletion.

### Version Information

42d9cf22d8a25fd5b279dd7559f972b142c3d45e

### Steps to Reproduce

  1. Create a bookmark (conversation tag).
  2. Open the bookmarks table and delete the bookmark.
  3. Observe an error toast: “There was an error deleting the bookmark” even though the bookmark is removed.

### What browsers are you seeing the problem on?

Chrome

### Relevant log output

```shell
Server logs: no relevant errors in ./api/logs/* while reproducing.
  Browser console: no actionable errors observed during reproduction.
  Behavior traced to client cache update logic: deleting a tag attempts to clone an undefined
  allConversations cache, which can throw synchronously and trigger the error toast.
```

danny-avila/LibreChat#10197.body:
### What features would you like to see added?

### Problem Description
Instance administrators currently lack the ability to edit or remove agents and prompts that have been shared globally by non-admin users in the Agent Marketplace and Prompt Library. This creates a critical governance gap for organizations with legal and data privacy compliance requirements.

### Current Behavior
1. Non-admin users can share agents globally via the Agent Marketplace
2. Non-admin users can share prompts globally via "Share with everyone" feature
3. Admin users cannot edit or delete these globally shared resources, even with database admin privileges
4. The "Share with everyone" feature cannot be disabled via configuration after the v0.8.0 update
5. Admins have no oversight or control over what content gets shared organization-wide

### Expected Behavior
Database administrators should automatically receive owner-level permissions for all globally shared content, including:
- Ability to edit any globally shared agent
- Ability to delete any globally shared agent
- Ability to edit any globally shared prompt
- Ability to delete any globally shared prompt
- Ability to disable global sharing features via environment variables

### Business Impact
**Legal and Compliance Risk:**
- Legal/privacy departments may identify agents or prompts that violate data protection regulations (GDPR, AI Act, etc.)
- Cannot remove non-compliant content without direct database manipulation
- No administrative control over organization-wide content distribution

**Current Workaround:**
We have completely disabled the Agent Marketplace and Agent Builder features for all users, significantly reducing LibreChat's value proposition.

### Proposed Solution
1. **Auto-grant admin ownership:** When content is shared globally, automatically add all instance admins as co-owners with full edit/delete permissions
2. **Admin override capabilities:** Admins should be able to manage any shared content regardless of original ownership
3. **Configuration controls:** Add environment variables to control sharing features: AGENTS_ALLOW_GLOBAL_SHARING=true/false
PROMPTS_ALLOW_GLOBAL_SHARING=true/false

### More details

### Environment
- LibreChat Version: v0.8.0+
- Deployment: Self-hosted with MongoDB

### Additional Context
This issue affects enterprise deployments where governance, compliance, and content moderation are critical requirements. Without administrative controls, many organizations cannot enable these valuable features.

### Related
This impacts both the Agent Marketplace (introduced recently) and the Prompt Library's global sharing functionality.

### Which components are impacted by your request?

General

danny-avila/LibreChat#10284.body: ### What happened? When using an **Ollama endpoint with a model that produces reasoning tags (e.g., `qwen3:8b`)**, the system is incorrectly saving the entire LLM reasoning block as the chat's title. This includes the internal `...` tags, instead of just the final, sanitized title output. This behavior was *not* observed with other models (like Llama 3), suggesting the bug is in how LibreChat parses the response from specific models that output reasoning blocks. This results in an extremely long and improper string being used as the title. When attempting to delete such a chat, this long string is displayed within the "Delete chat?" confirmation modal. For example: `This will delete Okay, the user sent "hi" and the AI responded with a greeting... [full reasoning] ... User Hi Greeting ?` The excessive length of this string causes the modal's content area to expand vertically beyond its bounds, pushing the "Cancel" and "Delete" buttons outside the visible area and making them unclickable. **Expected Behavior:** 1. **Core/Backend Fix:** The title generation logic must be corrected to properly parse the output from reasoning models like Qwen. It must **only save the final, intended title** (e.g., "User Hi Greeting") as the conversation title. All internal processing tags (`.../think>`) must be stripped and not saved. 2. **UI/Frontend Fix:** As a defensive measure, the title text rendered within the delete confirmation modal should be **gracefully truncated** (e.g., using CSS `truncate`). This ensures that even if a title is unexpectedly long, it will not break the modal's layout. ### Version Information **Version Information:** - LibreChat Version: v0.8.0 - Docker Image Info: Latest **Environment:** - Browser: Brave 1.83.112 (Official Build) (64-bit) - OS: Fedora 41 ### Steps to Reproduce **Steps to Reproduce:** 1. Configure LibreChat to use an **Ollama endpoint**. 2. Start a new chat using a model known to output reasoning tags (e.g., **`qwen3:8b`**). 3. Send a message (e.g., "hi") to trigger automatic title generation. 4. After the response, check the conversation list in the sidebar. Note that the title is not a short string, but the entire LLM reasoning block (e.g., `...User Hi Greeting`). 5. Hover over this chat item and click the "Delete" button. 6. Observe the "Delete chat?" confirmation modal. 7. Note that the long, reasoning-filled title is displayed within the modal. 8. Observe that the modal has expanded downwards, hiding the "Delete" button. ### What browsers are you seeing the problem on? _No response_ ### Relevant log output ```shell - Backend Logs: No relevant errors observed. - Browser Developer Console (F12 > Console): No JavaScript or layout errors were observed. ``` ### Screenshots Screenshot 1: Shows the delete confirmation modal functioning correctly for a chat with a standard short title ("Short Title"). Screenshot 2 : Shows the source of the kind of long text (including tags) in the main chat window that, when used as a title, causes the modal overflow issue. ### Code of Conduct - [x] I agree to follow this project's Code of Conduct

danny-avila/LibreChat#10334.body:
### What happened?

The "upload to provider" feature visibility is controlled by isDocumentSupportedProvider() which checks against a hardcoded list in `packages/data-provider/src/schemas.ts:60-93`:

```
  export const documentSupportedProviders = new Set<string>([
    EModelEndpoint.anthropic,
    EModelEndpoint.openAI,  // ← "openAI" is here
    EModelEndpoint.custom,
    EModelEndpoint.azureOpenAI,
    // ... etc
  ]);
```

  The issue: When using a LiteLLM gateway:
  1. Your agent's provider field contains the custom endpoint name (e.g., "litellm" or your gateway's name)
  2. The backend correctly treats it as OpenAI-compatible (sets overrideProvider = Providers.OPENAI in api/server/services/Endpoints/index.js:73)
  3. BUT the frontend checks using the original provider name, not the overridden one
  4. Since "litellm" isn't in the documentSupportedProviders set, the upload option is hidden

Commit ff027e824 (Oct 9, 2025) changed the check priority in `client/src/components/Chat/Input/Files/AttachFileMenu.tsx:123:`

```
  - if (isDocumentSupportedProvider(endpointType || currentProvider)) {
  + if (isDocumentSupportedProvider(currentProvider || endpointType)) {
```

This now checks currentProvider (your gateway name) first instead of falling back to endpointType. This was likely an intentional fix for direct provider attachments, but it broke OpenAI-compatible gateways.

### Version Information

d084f447ab68e4514675e0dafd96bfe27ac6118d

### Steps to Reproduce

1. Configure a LiteLLM endpoint (or any other type of custom endpoint) pointing to an OpenAI model
2. Check that Upload to Provider does not appear

### What browsers are you seeing the problem on?

N/A
```

danny-avila/LibreChat#10493.body: ### What happened? I believe following #10414, when files are uploaded using "Upload as text" to a non-agent chat (eg a modelSpec, or just choosing an endpoint and model directly), the standard OCR/context processing doesn't happen, and the file isn't attached to the message (disappears from the UI after sending the message, and the model doesn't receive the file content) Files uploaded using "Upload as text" now no longer seem to hit the `processAgentFileUpload()` method in `api/server/services/Files/process.js`, I think due to the endpoint in the upload POST no longer being `agents`. Before this commit, when POSTing to /api/files, the payload contains: ``` endpoint= agents original_endpoint= openAI message_file= true tool_resouce= context ``` Which gets a response: ```json { "message": "Agent file uploaded and processed successfully", "_id": "69167389e5f57cf920f63d1e", "file_id": "b97ea372-b3ee-499b-b219-a9538ae61618", "__v": 0, "bytes": 1340, "context": "message_attachment", "createdAt": "2025-11-14T00:10:49.359Z", "filename": "new 46.txt", "filepath": "/app/uploads/temp/678db103cfeea53d4ee7f526/new_46.txt", "object": "file", "source": "text", "temp_file_id": "9a48b60f-4cfb-4310-8702-81409d51a981", "text": "content of my file", "type": "text/plain", "updatedAt": "2025-11-14T00:10:49.359Z", "usage": 0, "user": "678db103cfeea53d4ee7f526" } ``` And on the current version, the POST payload contains: ``` endpoint= openAI endpointType= message_file= true tool_resouce= context ``` And gets a response: ```json { "message": "File uploaded and processed successfully", "_id": "6916741240488ec6460b13d1", "file_id": "41cea68f-83bf-4b2e-9e94-c3a158100827", "updatedAt": "2025-11-14T00:13:06.885Z", "source": "local", "type": "text/plain", "context": "message_attachment", "filename": "new_46.txt", "filepath": "/uploads/6736c195266216764eecdf43/41cea68f-83bf-4b2e-9e94-c3a158100827__new_46.txt", "bytes": 1353, "temp_file_id": "935065fc-d3bc-41ed-8863-07694cd035ac", "user": "6736c195266216764eecdf43", "__v": 0, "createdAt": "2025-11-14T00:13:06.885Z", "object": "file", "usage": 0 } ``` When sending the chat message with this text file attached, the text file disappears from the UI, and the model doesn't receive the file content. The text file doesn't have the little "T" icon indicating it was uploaded as text context either. ### Version Information f6868fc851c55482f6a3f6a463cf40a3c4202142 ### Steps to Reproduce 1. ### What features would you like to see added?

The google endpoint should be included in the supportsBalanceCheck configuration to enable proper token balance tracking and auto-refill functionality for Google/Vertex AI models (including Gemini and Claude models accessed via Vertex AI).

### More details

Currently, when users use models through the google endpoint, their token balance is not checked or deducted, and the auto-refill mechanism never triggers. This causes:

- Token balance remaining at 0 even after the refill interval passes
- Auto-refill only triggering when users switch to a different endpoint (like Azure OpenAI/GPT models)
- Inconsistent balance behavior across different endpoints

Proposed Fix: Add `EModelEndpoint.google: true` to the `supportsBalanceCheck` object:

```
export const supportsBalanceCheck = {
  [EModelEndpoint.custom]: true,
  [EModelEndpoint.openAI]: true,
  [EModelEndpoint.google]: true,  // <-- Add this line
  [EModelEndpoint.anthropic]: true,
  [EModelEndpoint.gptPlugins]: true,
  [EModelEndpoint.assistants]: true,
  [EModelEndpoint.agents]: true,
  [EModelEndpoint.azureAssistants]: true,
  [EModelEndpoint.azureOpenAI]: true,
  [EModelEndpoint.bedrock]: true,
};
```

danny-avila/LibreChat#11012.body:
### What happened?

We are implementing the Memory feature. Our `librechat.yaml` file specifies mostly custom endpoints - we are hoping to retire the azureOpenAI endpoint (see [this discussion](https://github.com/danny-avila/LibreChat/discussions/10193#discussioncomment-14916218) for details).

When pointing the `memory.agent` configuration item to either the ID value of an agent pointing at a custom endpoint or directly to a custom endpoint via the `provider` and `model` items, custom headers configured under that custom endpoint which point to an environment variable are not getting expanded. In our implementation this causes authentication to fail, because we have to send over a custom `api-key` header. We have the following custom headers defined:

```
X-User-Identifier: "{{LIBRECHAT_USER_EMAIL}}"
X-Application-Identifier: "LibreChat - Dev"
api-key: "${LIBRECHAT_SUBSCRIPTION_KEY}"
```

Viewing our API gateway logs, the custom headers are getting sent over, but the ones which are pointing to environment variables are not getting expanded - it's just sending over the raw value.

In our scenario, this causing auth to fail for the memory calls.

As a temporary workaround, I've pointed the memory config to an azureOpenAI endpoint, as that is properly expanding environment variables.

### Version Information

da108155663f8aaab4e9d83e8a45aba273f0f7aa

### Steps to Reproduce

Set up a custom endpoint with custom headers defined which point to environment variables, something like this:

```
custom:
  - name: "Azure OpenAI - General Purpose"
    apiKey: "${LIBRECHAT_SUBSCRIPTION_KEY}"
    baseURL: "https://<custom_domain>/ai/azure-oaiv1"
    customParams:
      defaultParamsEndpoint: azureOpenAI
    headers:
      X-User-Identifier: "{{LIBRECHAT_USER_EMAIL}}"
      X-Application-Identifier: "LibreChat - Dev"
      api-key: "${LIBRECHAT_SUBSCRIPTION_KEY}"
    models:
      default:
        - "gpt-5-chat-2025-10-03"
        - "gpt-4.1-2025-04-14"
        - "gpt-4.1-mini-2025-04-14"
        - "gpt-4.1-nano-2025-04-14"
        - "gpt-4o-2024-11-20"
        - "gpt-4o-mini-2024-07-18"
      fetch: false
    summarize: false
    titleConvo: true
    titleModel: "gpt-4.1-nano-2025-04-14"
```

Then configure memory to point to this:

```
memory:
  disabled: false
  tokenLimit: 3000
  personalize: true
  messageWindowSize: 10
  agent:
    provider: "Azure OpenAI - General Purpose"
    model: "gpt-5-chat-2025-10-03"
```

### What browsers are you seeing the problem on?

_No response_

```shell
error: Memory Agent failed to process memory 401 status code (no body)
```

danny-avila/LibreChat#11055.body:
### What happened?

I maintain a popular MCP server and a contributor recently raised a [PR](https://github.com/taylorwilsdon/google_workspace_mcp/pull/308) with a shim fix that allows LibreChat to continue working with FastMCP v2.14.0 or later. FastMCP is the most popular python mcp sdk and rather than patch thousands of MCP servers individually with 300+ lines of code, it seems prudent to resolve the issue at the source by introducing compliant token payloads. 

It should be a simple fix - we simply need to ensure in `packages/api/src/mcp/oauth/handler.ts` that server-specific `oauth_headers` no longer override SDK-required headers during token exchange and ensure `Content-Type` stays `application/x-www-form-urlencoded` and `Authorization` (Basic/client_id) isn’t clobbered, preserving OAuth 2.0/2.1 compliance for `/token` requests.

Should be as simple as:
```diff
diff --git a/packages/api/src/mcp/oauth/handler.ts b/packages/api/src/mcp/oauth/handler.ts
index 7986e2ee5..ec9325eb8 100644
--- a/packages/api/src/mcp/oauth/handler.ts
+++ b/packages/api/src/mcp/oauth/handler.ts
@@ -33,8 +33,11 @@ export class MCPOAuthHandler {
   private static createOAuthFetch(headers: Record<string, string>): FetchLike {
     return async (url: string | URL, init?: RequestInit): Promise<Response> => {
       const newHeaders = new Headers(init?.headers ?? {});
       for (const [key, value] of Object.entries(headers)) {
-        newHeaders.set(key, value);
+        if (!newHeaders.has(key)) {
+          newHeaders.set(key, value);
+        }
       }
```

### Version Information

```
➜  LibreChat git:(main) git rev-parse HEAD

cd2813d5d7ebf19135bec99d46a32e731c00fdc7
```

### Steps to Reproduce

https://github.com/taylorwilsdon/google_workspace_mcp/pull/308

### What browsers are you seeing the problem on?

Chrome

`https://github.com/taylorwilsdon/google_workspace_mcp/pull/308`

danny-avila/LibreChat#11058.body:
### Description
There is a visual regression in the Right Sidebar (Agent Builder/Settings) animation logic. When switching between different tabs (e.g., from "Prompts" to "Memories"), the content of the exiting tab does not clear before the new tab's content appears.

Instead of a clean cross-fade or a "wait-then-enter" transition, both views render simultaneously in the same layout space. This results in text, buttons, and input fields from the previous view overlapping with the new view, creating a cluttered and unreadable UI for the duration of the animation (approx. 300-500ms).

### Steps to Reproduce
1. Open the Right Sidebar.
2. Click on any content-heavy tab (e.g., **"Prompts"**).
3. Wait for it to fully load.
4. Click on a different tab (e.g., **"Erinnerungen" / "Memories"**).
5. Observe the content area during the transition.

### Expected Behavior
The interface should use a distinct transition mode to prevent visual collision.
*   **Preferred:** The exiting component should disappear immediately or fade out completely *before* the new component enters the DOM (e.g., Framer Motion `mode="wait"`).
*   **Alternative:** If a cross-fade is desired, the entering component should have a solid background to cover the exiting component, or the exiting component should be removed from the document flow immediately to prevent layout shifts/overlaps.

### Actual Behavior
The new content renders directly on top of (or blended with) the fading old content.
*   Text from the old tab (e.g., "malron test", "Verwalten") remains readable underneath the new tab's headers.
*   Input fields and buttons collision make the UI look broken until the animation settles.

### Visual Evidence / Frame Analysis
*Reference: `00:02` in the attached reproduction video.*

*   **Exiting View:** "Prompts" tab containing the search bar, the prompt item "malron test," and the "Verwalten" (Manage) button.
*   **Entering View:** "Memories" tab containing the filter input and "No memories" placeholder.
*   **The Glitch:** The "Verwalten" button from the old view is clearly visible *underneath* the "Erinnerungen" list item of the new view. The text "malron test" overlaps with the new search/filter input field.

### Environment
*   **LibreChat Version:** v0.8.2-rc1
*   **Browser:** Google Chrome (Windows)
*   **OS:** Windows 10/11

### Possible Fix (Dev Note)
The animation logic likely uses a library like **Framer Motion** or **React Transition Group**.
*   If using `<AnimatePresence>`, check if `mode="wait"` is missing. Adding `mode="wait"` ensures the exit animation finishes before the entry animation starts.
*   If a simultaneous cross-fade is intended (`mode="sync"`), ensure the container for the entering view has a higher `z-index` and a solid background color (e.g., `bg-white` or `bg-gray-900`) to mask the exiting content.

https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/c073d6b2-a5f1-47e4-a4fb-3730b872d66a
danny-avila/LibreChat#11058.title: [Bug]: Right Sidebar content visually overlaps/collides during tab transitions

danny-avila/LibreChat#11187.body: ### What happened? When a user does not explicitly set a value for `maxOutputTokens` in the Google/Gemini endpoint settings, LibreChat sends an empty string `"maxOutputTokens": ""` in the API request payload instead of omitting the field entirely. **Expected behavior:** If `maxOutputTokens` is not set by the user, the field should be omitted from the request payload, or at minimum not sent as an empty string. **Actual behavior:** The request payload contains `"generationConfig": {"stopSequences": [], "maxOutputTokens": "", ...}`. **Root cause analysis:** 1. `googleSchema` in `packages/data-provider/src/schemas.ts` calls `removeNullishValues(obj)` without passing `removeEmptyStrings=true`: ```typescript export const googleSchema = googleBaseSchema .transform((obj: Partial) => removeNullishValues(obj)) // missing second argument ``` 2. Similarly, getGoogleConfig() in packages/api/src/endpoints/google/llm.ts calls removeNullishValues() without the removeEmptyStrings flag. 3. While coerceNumber correctly transforms empty strings to undefined, there appear to be code paths where empty strings bypass this transformation. 4. The Gemini SDK uses != null to check field presence, so empty strings pass through and get sent to the API. Additional context: This issue also affects users of AI reverse proxies/middleware that expect maxOutputTokens to be a number type. When these proxies receive the empty string value, they fail to parse the request, causing errors. Fixing this would improve compatibility with the broader ecosystem of Gemini-compatible API proxies. ### Version Information 211b39f3113d4e6ecab84be0a83f4e9c9dea127f ### Steps to Reproduce 1. Configure an agent with Google/Gemini endpoint 2. Start a new conversation using Gemini model 3. Do NOT manually set maxOutputTokens in the endpoint settings (leave it at default/unset) 4. Send a message 5. Inspect the outgoing API request using a proxy tool (e.g., mitmproxy, Fiddler, Charles) 6. Observe that the request body contains "maxOutputTokens": "" instead of omitting the field ### What browsers are you seeing the problem on? _No response_ ### Relevant log output ```shell Captured request payload via MITM proxy: { "generationConfig": { "stopSequences": [], "maxOutputTokens": "", ... }, ... } Expected payload should either omit maxOutputTokens entirely or contain a valid number: { "generationConfig": { "stopSequences": [] }, ... } ``` ### Screenshots _No response_ ### Code of Conduct - [x] I agree to follow this project's Code of Conduct
danny-avila/LibreChat#11187.title: [Bug]: LibreChat send empty `maxOutputTokens` to Gemini Upstream

danny-avila/LibreChat#11301.body:
## What happened?

The GeminiImageGen tool fails with `TypeError: fetch failed sending request` when running in a Docker environment that requires HTTP proxy access to reach external APIs. Other image generation tools (DALLE3, OpenAI Image Tools, Flux API) work correctly in the same environment because they explicitly configure proxy support.

**Expected behavior:** The GeminiImageGen tool should work in environments that require HTTP proxy for external API access, consistent with other image generation tools.

**Root Cause Analysis:**

1. **Proxy works for other services:** Verified that the proxy is functioning correctly - OpenAI API requests show `via: 1.1 ... (squid)` in response headers, confirming requests go through the proxy.

2. **Container can reach Google APIs via proxy:**
   ```bash
   # With proxy - SUCCESS (HTTP 404 means endpoint was reached)
   docker exec LibreChat-API curl -x http://<proxy>:<port> https://global-aiplatform.googleapis.com
   # Returns: 404

   # Without proxy - TIMEOUT (no direct internet access)
   docker exec LibreChat-API curl --noproxy '*' https://global-aiplatform.googleapis.com
   # Returns: exit code 28 (timeout)
   ```

3. **Other image tools have proxy support:** Tools like `DALLE3.js` and `OpenAIImageTools.js` explicitly configure proxy:
   ```javascript
   // From DALLE3.js (lines 45-51)
   if (process.env.PROXY) {
     const proxyAgent = new ProxyAgent(process.env.PROXY);
     config.fetchOptions = {
       dispatcher: proxyAgent,
     };
   }
   ```

4. **GeminiImageGen.js lacks proxy support:** The tool initializes the `@google/genai` client without any proxy configuration:
   ```javascript
   // From GeminiImageGen.js (lines 123-127)
   return new GoogleGenAI({
     vertexai: true,
     project: serviceKey.project_id,
     location: process.env.GOOGLE_LOC || process.env.GOOGLE_CLOUD_LOCATION || 'global',
   });
   // No httpOptions or proxy configuration!
   ```

5. **Node.js native fetch doesn't respect HTTP_PROXY:** Node.js 20's native `fetch()` (powered by undici) does NOT automatically respect `HTTP_PROXY`/`HTTPS_PROXY` environment variables. A `dispatcher` option with a `ProxyAgent` must be explicitly passed.

**Proposed Fix:**
Add proxy support to `GeminiImageGen.js` similar to other image tools. The `@google/genai` SDK supports `httpOptions` which can be configured with a custom fetch dispatcher.

**Note:** The `@google/genai` SDK v1.33.0 may not expose a way to pass a custom dispatcher to its internal `fetch()` calls. If so, this may require:
- Opening an issue with the `@google/genai` SDK team to add proxy/httpAgent support
- Using a workaround like `global-agent` to globally intercept fetch calls
- Using a custom fetch implementation via `httpOptions.fetch` if supported

**Workaround:**
Currently, there is no configuration-only workaround. Users requiring proxy access must either:
- Use a different image generation tool (DALLE3, OpenAI Image Gen)
- Build a custom Docker image with `global-agent` pre-loaded
- Configure transparent proxy at the network level

## Version Information

- **LibreChat Version:** v0.8.2-rc2
- **@google/genai SDK Version:** 1.33.0
- **Node.js Version:** 20.x
- **Deployment:** Docker (`ghcr.io/danny-avila/librechat-dev-api:latest`)

**Configuration:**
```env
# Proxy settings (required for external API access)
PROXY=http://<proxy>:<port>
HTTP_PROXY=http://<proxy>:<port>
HTTPS_PROXY=http://<proxy>:<port>

# Gemini Image Generation
GEMINI_VERTEX_ENABLED=true
GEMINI_IMAGE_MODEL=gemini-3-pro-image-preview
GOOGLE_LOC=global
```

## Steps to Reproduce

1. Deploy LibreChat in a Docker environment that requires HTTP proxy for external internet access
2. Configure environment variables: `PROXY`, `HTTP_PROXY`, `HTTPS_PROXY` pointing to the proxy server
3. Configure Gemini Image Generation with Vertex AI credentials
4. Create an agent with the GeminiImageGen tool enabled
5. Send a message requesting image generation (e.g., "Generate an image of a sunset")
6. Observe the "fetch failed" error

## Relevant log output

```json
{
  "level": "error",
  "message": "[GeminiImageGen] API error: exception TypeError: fetch failed sending request",
  "stack": "Error: exception TypeError: fetch failed sending request\n    at /app/node_modules/@google/genai/dist/node/index.cjs:11894:19\n    at process.processTicksAndRejections (node:internal/process/task_queues:95:5)\n    at async Models.generateContent (/app/node_modules/@google/genai/dist/node/index.cjs:13107:24)\n    at async tool (/app/api/app/clients/tools/structured/GeminiImageGen.js:453:23)"
}
```

## Related Files

- `api/app/clients/tools/structured/GeminiImageGen.js` - Missing proxy support
- `api/app/clients/tools/structured/DALLE3.js` - Reference implementation with proxy support
- `api/app/clients/tools/structured/OpenAIImageTools.js` - Reference implementation with proxy support

### What happened?

When using Claude models through AWS Bedrock with extended thinking enabled, LibreChat fails with `MESSAGE_COERCION_FAILURE` errors. The issue occurs when Claude returns `reasoning_content` blocks before tool calls.

Extended thinking enabled

LibreChat should properly handle `reasoning_content` blocks returned by Claude via AWS Bedrock, similar to how it handles them via the direct Anthropic API.

danny-avila/LibreChat#11431.body:
### What happened?

### Issue
When MCP server configuration includes user placeholders like `{{LIBRECHAT_USER_NAME}}` with non-ASCII characters (e.g., "Marić", "José", "Müller"), the application crashes with a ByteString conversion error.

### Error Message
```
Cannot convert argument to a ByteString because the character at 
index X has a value of 263 which is greater than 255.
```

### Root Cause
HTTP headers are restricted to ASCII characters (byte values 0-255) per the Fetch API standard. Characters like:
- ć (U+0107 = 263) ❌ Fails
- ñ (U+00F1 = 241) ✅ Works (< 256)
- ü (U+00FC = 252) ✅ Works (< 256)

The current code in `packages/api/src/utils/env.ts` line 98:
```typescript
const replacementValue = fieldValue == null ? '' : String(fieldValue);
```

This passes non-ASCII characters directly to the `Headers` API without encoding, causing crashes for characters with Unicode values > 255.

### Impact
- Users with names containing Slavic characters (ć, đ, ł, š, ž, etc.) cannot use MCP servers
- Affects international users globally
- Blocks MCP server integration for affected users

### Version Information

d2b3459ae1536b91c893db50efd092e2ce33c8d0

### Steps to Reproduce

1. **Set up an MCP server** that expects user information in headers (e.g., `X-User-Name`)

2. **Configure LibreChat** to use the MCP server with user placeholders in `librechat.yaml`:
   ```yaml
   mcpServers:
     my-server:
       type: sse
       url: http://localhost:5003/sse
       headers:
         X-User-Name: "{{LIBRECHAT_USER_NAME}}"
   ```

3. **Create a user account** with a name containing Unicode characters > 255:
   - Examples: "Đorđe" (Đ = 272), "Marić" (ć = 263), "Łukasz" (Ł = 321)

4. **Login** with that user account

5. **Attempt to use the MCP server** (e.g., invoke any MCP tool)

6. **Observe the error:**
   ```
   Cannot convert argument to a ByteString because the character at 
   index X has a value of [263/272/etc] which is greater than 255.
   ```

7. **Result:** Application crashes and MCP server cannot be used

### What browsers are you seeing the problem on?

Microsoft Edge, Chrome

### Relevant log output

```shell
Cannot convert argument to a ByteString because the character at index 15 has a value of 263 which is greater than 255.
```

### Screenshots

_No response_

danny-avila/LibreChat#11550.body:
## Description

The Vertex AI integration explicitly filters out the `context-1m-2025-08-07` beta header in the `filterVertexHeaders` function, preventing users from utilizing the 1M context window with Claude Sonnet 4/4.5 on Vertex AI.

**However, Vertex AI DOES support 1M context** - this has been proven by:
1. [Roo-Code PR #8685](https://github.com/RooCodeInc/Roo-Code/pull/8685) - working implementation that sends the header to Vertex AI
2. [Anthropic's official documentation](https://platform.claude.com/docs/en/build-with-claude/context-windows) explicitly states: "The 1M token context window is currently available on the Claude API, Microsoft Foundry, Amazon Bedrock, and **Google Cloud's Vertex AI**"
3. Google's Model Garden page for Claude Sonnet 4.5 lists "1M token context window (beta)" as a supported feature

## Affected Code

[`packages/api/src/endpoints/anthropic/vertex.ts` line 24](https://github.com/danny-avila/LibreChat/blob/25fe4069a46ed8291ecb24954bc70191353c3e4d/packages/api/src/endpoints/anthropic/vertex.ts#L24)

```typescript
if (v.includes('context-1m')) {
  return false;  // This incorrectly filters out the header
}
```

## Steps to Reproduce

1. Configure LibreChat with Anthropic via Vertex AI
2. Start a conversation and build up context beyond 200k tokens
3. Receive "413 Prompt is too long" error

## Expected Behavior

The `context-1m-2025-08-07` beta header should be forwarded to Vertex AI, allowing 1M context conversations.

## Actual Behavior

The header is filtered out, limiting conversations to ~200k tokens.

## Workaround

Use direct Anthropic API instead of Vertex AI - the `context-1m` header is correctly forwarded when not using Vertex AI.

## Proposed Fix

Remove `context-1m` from the `filterVertexHeaders` function's exclusion list, as Vertex AI now supports this beta feature.

## Environment

- LibreChat version: v0.8.2-rc2
- Provider: Google Cloud Vertex AI
- Model: claude-sonnet-4-5@20250929

## Additional Context

We tested this extensively:
- With Vertex AI: Limited to 200k tokens, gets "Prompt is too long" error
- With direct Anthropic API: Successfully handled 400k+ token conversations

The filter was likely added when Vertex AI didn't support 1M context, but Google has since added support for this beta feature.
danny-avila/LibreChat#11550.title: [Bug]: Vertex AI integration filters out context-1m beta header, blocking 1M context support

danny-avila/LibreChat#11609.body:
## What happened?

After upgrading to commits after v0.8.2 (specifically after PR #11588), files uploaded for code execution are no longer visible to the agent. Files upload successfully to the agent's code files section, but when the AI attempts to access them, it reports `/mnt/data/` as empty.

**Expected behavior:** The agent should see uploaded files in `/mnt/data/` and the system message should include:
```
- Note: The following files are available in the "execute_code" tool environment:
    - /mnt/data/<filename>
```

**Actual behavior:** The agent reports the directory is empty and the system message is missing the file availability note.

**Root Cause:** PR #11588 (`🦥 refactor: Event-Driven Lazy Tool Loading`) made `primeResources` non-mutating, but callers weren't updated to use the returned values:
1. `initializeAgent` doesn't return the primed `tool_resources`
2. `loadToolDefinitionsWrapper` doesn't prime files or generate `toolContextMap`
3. `loadToolsForExecution` discards `toolContextMap` from `loadTools`

## Version Information

Any commit after v0.8.2 (6960bd3cc), specifically after PR #11588 (5af1342db)

```
git rev-parse HEAD
# 9f23dd38e or later
```

## Steps to Reproduce

1. Create or select an agent with the `execute_code` tool enabled
2. Upload a file to the agent's code files section
3. Send a message asking the agent to list files in `/mnt/data/`
4. Observe: Agent incorrectly reports the directory as empty

## Relevant log output

No errors in logs - the issue is that the system message sent to the AI is missing the file context that should be injected by `toolContextMap[Tools.execute_code]`.

The debug log will show the agent being initialized but the `toolContextMap` will be empty `{}` instead of containing the execute_code file context.

## Code of Conduct

Remove deprecated Claude models (claude-1, claude-2, early claude-3-2024, claude-instant) from model configuration lists. These have been superseded by newer versions and should no longer appear in model selection.

**Files:** `packages/data-provider/src/config.ts`
**Models:** claude-1, claude-1.2, claude-2, claude-2.1, claude-instant-1, claude-3-*-20240229, claude-3-*-20240307, and corresponding bedrock models

### More details

### Background
Anthropic has deprecated several older Claude models in favor of newer, more capable versions. These deprecated models are still listed in LibreChat's configuration, which can:
- Confuse users when selecting models
- Lead to API errors if users try to use unavailable models
- Clutter the model selection interface
- Create maintenance burden for outdated model configurations

### Detailed List of Models to Remove

**From `sharedAnthropicModels` array in `packages/data-provider/src/config.ts`:**

Legacy Claude 1.x models:
- `claude-1`
- `claude-1.2`
- `claude-1-100k`

Legacy Claude Instant models:
- `claude-instant-1`
- `claude-instant-1-100k`

Legacy Claude 2.x models:
- `claude-2`
- `claude-2.1`

Early 2024 Claude 3.x models (superseded by newer versions):
- `claude-3-opus-20240229`
- `claude-3-sonnet-20240229`
- `claude-3-haiku-20240307`

**From `bedrockModels` array in `packages/data-provider/src/config.ts`:**

Early 2024 Claude 3.x Bedrock models:
- `anthropic.claude-3-haiku-20240307-v1:0`
- `anthropic.claude-3-opus-20240229-v1:0`
- `anthropic.claude-3-sonnet-20240229-v1:0`

Legacy Bedrock models:
- `anthropic.claude-instant-v1`
- `anthropic.claude-v2`
- `anthropic.claude-v2:1`

### Replacement Models Available

Users should migrate to these current models:
- **Claude 4.x**: claude-sonnet-4-5, claude-haiku-4-5, claude-opus-4-5
- **Claude 3.7**: claude-3-7-sonnet-latest
- **Claude 3.5**: claude-3-5-sonnet-latest, claude-3-5-haiku-20241022

### Implementation Details

**File to modify:**
- `packages/data-provider/src/config.ts` (lines ~1110-1159)

**What to do:**
1. Remove the deprecated model strings from `sharedAnthropicModels` array
2. Remove the deprecated model strings from `bedrockModels` array
3. Ensure proper array formatting and trailing commas

**What NOT to change:**
- Test files that reference these models (they test legacy behavior)
- Pattern-based references in pricing (`api/models/tx.js`) - these use pattern matching
- Pattern-based references in token limits (`packages/api/src/utils/tokens.ts`) - these use pattern matching

### Testing Requirements

Before submitting PR:
- [ ] Run `npm run test:api` - all tests should pass
- [ ] Run `npm run test:client` - all tests should pass
- [ ] Run `npm run lint` - no linting errors
- [ ] Run `npm run build:data-provider` - build should succeed
- [ ] Verify deprecated models no longer appear in model selection UI
- [ ] Test that existing conversations using these models are handled gracefully (may show error or fallback)

### Potential Impact

**User Impact:**
- Users with presets/conversations using deprecated models will need to update their model selection
- Model selection dropdown will be cleaner with only current models

**Breaking Changes:**
- Any hardcoded references to these exact model names will need updating
- Presets saved with these models will need to be updated by users

### Migration Path

Users currently using deprecated models should:
1. Update their presets to use newer model versions
2. Update any saved conversations that reference deprecated models
3. The system should handle this gracefully (may show warnings or errors)

### References

- Anthropic API documentation on model deprecation
- Current model availability: https://docs.anthropic.com/claude/docs/models-overview
- LibreChat model configuration: `packages/data-provider/src/config.ts`

danny-avila/LibreChat#11648.body:
### What happened?

  Summary:                                                                                                                                         
  The /api/mcp/connection/status response is computed on demand per API process and is not persisted. It uses in‑memory MCPManager connection maps 
  (app + user connections) that live inside each container. As a result, different API containers can return different status for the same user/   
  server at the same time.                                                                                                                         
                                                                                                                                                   
  Where it’s computed:                                                                                                                             
                                                                                                                                                   
  - Route: api/server/routes/mcp.js                                                                                                                
  - Logic: api/server/services/MCP.js                                                                                                              
  - Connection state: packages/api/src/mcp/ConnectionsRepository.ts (per‑process Maps)                                                             
                                                                                                                                                   
  Storage:                                                                                                                                         
  None. Status is not stored in DB/Redis.                                                                                                          
                                                                                                                                                   
  Impact:                                                                                                                                          
  In a multi‑container setup (no sticky sessions), users get inconsistent /status results depending on which container handles the request.        
                                                                                                                                                   
  Implication / Fix Options:                                                                                                                       
                                                                                                                                                   
  - Use sticky sessions for client → API requests (so the same user hits the same container). Which we would love to avoid                                                     
  - Or implement shared connection/status storage so all containers report consistent status.  Which I'm not sure actually work for non-stateless mcp servers

Response appears truncated Impact - Responses can be cut off unexpectedly - It happens when Redis resumable streams are enabled ### Version Information latest ### Steps to Reproduce Prereqs - .env contains: - USE_REDIS_STREAMS=true Steps 1. danny-avila/LibreChat#11762.body:
### What happened?

The [resumable streams](https://www.librechat.ai/docs/features/resumable_streams) feature [introduced in v0.8.2](https://www.librechat.ai/changelog/v0.8.2#resumable-llm-streams-with-horizontal-scaling) is causing our Redis instance to run out of memory and impact LibreChat. Job metadata hashes (`::stream:*:job`) are created without TTL expiration, causing them to accumulate indefinitely until Redis hits maxmemory and triggers OOM errors. To mitigate, we are implementing a custom automated process to remove the job hashes at a regular interval outside of LibreChat.

- **LibreChat Version:** `v0.8.2`
- **Resumable Streams Configuration:**
  - `USE_REDIS=true`
  - `USE_REDIS_STREAMS=true`
- **Deployment:**
  - Local: Docker 
    - Redis Version: `6.2.21`
  - Production: AWS ElastiCache 
    - Node Size: `t3.medium`
    - Redis Version: `6.2.6`
    - Cluster mode: `Disabled`

### Version Information

[ghcr.io/danny-avila/librechat:v0.8.2](https://github.com/danny-avila/LibreChat/pkgs/container/librechat/661657998?tag=v0.8.2)

### Steps to Reproduce

1. Clear the redis database with `FLUSHDB` or similar
2. Create one or more conversations in LibreChat and wait for the responses to complete
3. Connect to Redis instance (i.e. using `redis-cli`)
4. Check for `::stream:*:job` hashes with -1 TTL
5. Repeat steps 1-5 and observe that `::stream:*:job` keys continue to accumulate

Example [redis-cli](https://redis.io/docs/latest/develop/tools/cli/) commands to inspect the job hashes using [flushdb](https://redis.io/docs/latest/commands/flushdb/), [keys](https://redis.io/docs/latest/commands/keys/), [ttl](https://redis.io/docs/latest/commands/ttl/), and [hgetall](https://redis.io/docs/latest/commands/hgetall/):

```
redis-cli -h localhost -p 6379 -a password
> FLUSHDB
> KEYS ::stream:*:job
> TTL ::stream:[SAMPLE_ID]:job
> HGETALL ::stream:[SAMPLE_ID]:job
```

Observe that job hashes are not automatically cleaned up like all other `::stream*` keys.

### What browsers are you seeing the problem on?

Firefox, Chrome, Safari, Microsoft Edge, Mobile (iOS), Mobile (Android)

### Relevant log output

```shell
# Here's an example redis-cli session after creating 3 conversations. 
# Note that I ran FLUSHDB first, created the 3 conversations, and then proceeded to inspect the job hashes.

$ redis-cli -u redis://user:password@localhost:6379

localhost:6379> FLUSHDB
OK

localhost:6379> KEYS ::stream:*:job
1) "::stream:{d987e079-8e49-4d34-9d81-dda6d23f2555}:job"
2) "::stream:{37b019b6-3c64-4fef-9573-11050a0a7a1b}:job"
3) "::stream:{6a382f3b-2ae3-43a3-9a00-0da3c246798e}:job"

localhost:6379> TTL "::stream:{d987e079-8e49-4d34-9d81-dda6d23f2555}:job"
(integer) -1

localhost:6379> TTL "::stream:{37b019b6-3c64-4fef-9573-11050a0a7a1b}:job"
(integer) -1

localhost:6379> TTL "::stream:{6a382f3b-2ae3-43a3-9a00-0da3c246798e}:job"
(integer) -1

localhost:6379> HGETALL "::stream:{6a382f3b-2ae3-43a3-9a00-0da3c246798e}:job"
1) "finalEvent"
2) "{\"final\":true,\"conversation\":{...}}"
```

danny-avila/LibreChat#11808.body:
## Bug Description

When MongoDB has transient connectivity issues (network blips, high load, connection pool exhaustion), the entire Node.js process crashes with `process.exit(1)`. In our production environment we saw **5 crashes in ~43 minutes** during a period of elevated load, all with the same error:

```
There was an uncaught error: connection <N> to <host>:27017 timed out
```

Each restart triggers a full Meilisearch index sync (~636K messages in our case), which adds more load to MongoDB and creates a cascading crash loop that's hard to recover from without intervention.

## Root Cause

Two things combine to make this happen:

**1. No `mongoose.connection.on('error')` listener in `api/db/connect.js`**

Per [Mongoose error handling docs](https://mongoosejs.com/docs/connections.html#error-handling), after the initial connection is made, you should listen for error events on the connection. The MongoDB driver runs background SDAM (Server Discovery and Monitoring) to check connection health. When those checks detect a timeout or disconnect, it emits an `error` event on the Mongoose connection.

Without a listener, Node.js [EventEmitter throws](https://nodejs.org/api/events.html#error-events) the error as an uncaught exception. The current `connectDb()` function connects but never registers a connection error listener:

https://github.com/danny-avila/LibreChat/blob/main/api/db/connect.js#L67-L70

**2. `uncaughtException` handler in `api/server/index.js` doesn't handle MongoDB errors**

The handler at the bottom of `api/server/index.js` has patterns for GoogleGenerativeAI, Meilisearch (`fetch failed`), OpenAI, and `@librechat/agents` errors — but nothing for MongoDB/Mongoose errors. So MongoDB connection errors fall through to `process.exit(1)`:

https://github.com/danny-avila/LibreChat/blob/main/api/server/index.js#L208-L253

## Steps to Reproduce

1. Start LibreChat connected to a MongoDB instance
2. Simulate a transient MongoDB connectivity issue (e.g., restart mongod, or use `tc` to add network latency/packet loss, or just run under heavy load where the connection pool gets exhausted)
3. Wait for the MongoDB driver's background monitoring to detect the issue
4. Observe: the process crashes with the "connection timed out" uncaught error

## Suggested Fix

**In `api/db/connect.js`**, register a connection error listener before the `mongoose.connect()` call:

```javascript
mongoose.connection.on('error', (err) => {
  logger.error('[connectDb] MongoDB connection error:', err.message);
});
```

**In `api/server/index.js`**, add a MongoDB error pattern to the `uncaughtException` handler (as defense-in-depth), similar to the existing patterns for GoogleGenerativeAI, Meilisearch, etc:

```javascript
if (
  err.message &&
  (err.message.includes('timed out') ||
    err.message.includes('MongoNetworkError') ||
    err.message.includes('MongoServerSelectionError') ||
    err.message.includes('ECONNREFUSED') ||
    err.message.includes('ECONNRESET'))
) {
  logger.error(
    'A MongoDB connection error occurred. The app will continue running.',
    { message: err.message },
  );
  return;
}
```

## Environment

- LibreChat version: latest main
- MongoDB 7.x (Atlas)
- Node.js 20.x
- Running in Docker on Linux

Happy to open a PR for this if it's welcome.
danny-avila/LibreChat#11808.title: [Bug] MongoDB connection timeouts crash the Node.js process (missing connection error listener)

danny-avila/LibreChat#11977.body:
### What happened?

When uploading files via the agent file_search panel (`SidePanel/Agents/FileSearch.tsx`), file size validation uses the **active conversation's** endpoint config instead of `"agents"`.

**Example:** `fileConfig` has `default.fileSizeLimit: 10` and `agents.fileSizeLimit: 25`. User has an active chat on a custom endpoint (resolves to `default`). They open the agent panel and try to upload a 15MB file to file_search — rejected at 10MB.

**Root cause** in `useFileHandling.ts` line 54:

```ts
const endpoint = conversation?.endpoint ?? 'default';
```

This reads from the active chat conversation, not from the agent context. The validation at line 254 then calls `getEndpointFileConfig` with this wrong endpoint.

Meanwhile, `FileSearch.tsx` line 67 correctly uses `EModelEndpoint.agents` for its own disabled check — but this doesn't affect the upload validation path.

**Expected:** file_search uploads should validate against `fileConfig.endpoints.agents`.

**Suggested fix:** add an optional endpoint override to `useFileHandling`:

```ts
// useFileHandling.ts
const endpoint = params?.endpointOverride ?? conversation?.endpoint ?? 'default';

// FileSearch.tsx
useFileHandling({ ..., endpointOverride: EModelEndpoint.agents });
```


### Version Information

v0.8.2-rc2

### Steps to Reproduce

1. In `librechat.yaml` set `fileConfig` with different limits: `default.fileSizeLimit: 10`, `agents.fileSizeLimit: 25`
2. Open a chat conversation using any custom endpoint
3. Open Agents SidePanel → file_search → try uploading a file between 10-25MB
4. Upload is rejected with the default endpoint's 10MB limit instead of agents' 25MB

### What browsers are you seeing the problem on?

_No response_

### Relevant log output

```shell
No server-side logs — file is rejected client-side before upload.
The error toast shows the wrong file size limit.


Relevant source files:
- `client/src/components/SidePanel/Agents/FileSearch.tsx` (lines 45-48, 67-72)
- `client/src/hooks/Files/useFileHandling.ts` (lines 54, 254-258)
- `packages/data-provider/src/file-config.ts` (`getEndpointFileConfig`)
```

### Screenshots

### What happened?

I encountered a severe issue with the left sidebar's chronological sorting after resetting and synchronizing the Meilisearch index using the official script.

Because my search feature wasn't returning accurate results, I decided to clear the search index and ran the command:
npm run reset-meili-sync

After the script finished and I refreshed the Web UI, the left sidebar completely lost its historical chronological order.
Specifically:

Almost all historical chats were suddenly lumped together and displayed as if they were updated/created on the same day ("Today" / "Previous 7 Days").
My actual most recent chats (the ones I was currently working on) disappeared from the top of the sidebar, getting buried or hidden in the massive list.
These missing chats were still safely stored in MongoDB and indexed by Meilisearch.
If I use the Search function , find a "missing" recent chat, load it, and send a new prompt, the chat's updatedAt timestamp naturally refreshes, and the chat magically reappears at the very top of the left sidebar, restoring normal behavior for that specific thread.


### Version Information

Lirechat: 0.8.2
LibreChat setup: Docker Compose
Search: Meilisearch enabled
Database: MongoDB
Additional context (Root cause hypothesis)
It seems that the reset-meili-sync script (or the Mongoose schemas involved during the document fetching/saving process) is inadvertently touching or triggering a re-save on the updatedAt fields in MongoDB. Because the sidebar groups by updatedAt, it gets overwhelmed by hundreds of chats sharing the exact same timestamp (the moment the script was run), breaking the UI pagination/grouping logic and hiding genuinely recent queries.

Observe that the chronological grouping is broken (everything merged into a recent time-block), and actual recent conversations are pushed out of standard view.
Use the Meilisearch bar, search for a missing recent chat, and send a message in it.
Observe the sidebar: the interacted chat correctly takes its place at the top of the list again.
Expected behavior
Running npm run reset-meili-sync should be a read-only operation regarding MongoDB timestamps. It should purely rebuild the Meilisearch index without affecting the updatedAt metadata of the conversations. Historical chats should remain in their respective "Previous 30 days", "Previous year" blocks after the sync is complete.

### What happened?

When using Azure Entra ID as the OpenID provider with `OPENID_REQUIRED_ROLE_TOKEN_KIND=id` and `OPENID_REQUIRED_ROLE_PARAMETER_PATH=groups`, users who belong to **200+ security groups** trigger the [group overage claim](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/entra/identity-platform/access-token-claims-reference#groups-overage-claim). 

The existing `resolveGroupsFromOverage()` function correctly detects the overage condition and attempts to call Microsoft Graph (`/me/getMemberObjects`), but it **passes the raw `tokenset.access_token` directly** as the Bearer token. When `OPENID_SCOPE` includes a custom API scope (e.g. `api://<client-id>/.default`) following [this doc](https://www.librechat.ai/docs/configuration/authentication/OAuth2-OIDC/token-reuse) the access token's audience (`aud`) is the application itself **not** `https://graph.microsoft.com`. Graph API rejects this token with **HTTP 401 Unauthorized**.



### Version Information

9a5d7eaa4ef1dbae21d06ea82e587c54e867b48b

### Steps to Reproduce

1. Configure Azure Entra ID as the OpenID provider with group claims enabled in the [app registration](https://www.librechat.ai/docs/configuration/authentication/OAuth2-OIDC/token-reuse)
2. Set the following `.env` variables:
   ```
   OPENID_REQUIRED_ROLE_TOKEN_KIND=id
   OPENID_REQUIRED_ROLE_PARAMETER_PATH=groups
   OPENID_REQUIRED_ROLE=<azure-ad-group-object-id>
   OPENID_SCOPE="api://<client-id>/.default openid profile email offline_access"
   ```
3. Have or simulate a user that belongs to **200+ Azure AD security groups** or force the overage manually.
4. Attempt to log in
5. The logs shows the overage failed.

Microsoft Edge

[openidStrategy] Detected group overage, resolving groups via Microsoft Graph getMemberObjects: https://graph.microsoft.com/v1.0/me/getMemberObjects

danny-avila/LibreChat#12298.body:
## Bug Description

Code execution with file attachments fails with **"socket hang up"** after exactly 5 seconds when running on **Node.js v23**. Code execution *without* files works fine for any duration (tested up to 45+ seconds).

### Reproduction

1. Attach at least one file to the code interpreter tool (so `tool_resources.execute_code.file_ids` is non-empty)
2. Run code that takes longer than 5 seconds (e.g. `import time; time.sleep(10); print("done")`)
3. The request fails with a socket hang up / `ECONNRESET` error at exactly 5000 ms

Code execution **without** files works indefinitely. Code execution **with** files that completes in under 5 seconds also works fine.

### Root Cause

Three independent factors combine to create the bug:

1. **`follow-redirects` v1.15.11 (used by axios) leaks `socket.destroy` on sockets.** In [`follow-redirects/index.js:209-213`](https://github.com/follow-redirects/follow-redirects/blob/main/index.js#L209-L213), `destroyOnTimeout` registers `socket.addListener("timeout", socket.destroy)` but the `clearTimer` cleanup (line 228) never removes it. The listener persists after the request completes.

2. **Node.js v23 changed `http.globalAgent` defaults** to `keepAlive: true`, `timeout: 5000`, and `scheduling: 'lifo'`. Sockets are now reused across requests by default, and a 5-second idle timeout is applied to all managed sockets.

3. **`primeFiles` (axios) and CodeExecutor (`node-fetch`) both connect to the same `CODE_BASEURL`**, causing socket reuse via the shared `http.globalAgent`.

### Why it only affects requests with file attachments

- **With files**: `primeFiles` runs and calls `getSessionInfo` (axios GET to `CODE_BASEURL/files/...`) and potentially `uploadCodeEnvFile` (axios POST to `CODE_BASEURL/upload`). These requests go through axios → `follow-redirects`, which leaks `socket.destroy` as a timeout handler on the TCP socket. With `keepAlive: true`, the socket is returned to `http.globalAgent`'s connection pool.

  When CodeExecutor's `node-fetch` subsequently POSTs to `CODE_BASEURL/exec`, Node.js reuses the pooled socket. After 5 seconds (`globalAgent.timeout`), the timeout event fires and the **leaked `socket.destroy` handler kills the active socket**.

- **Without files**: `primeFiles` returns early without making any HTTP requests to `CODE_BASEURL`. CodeExecutor creates a fresh socket with no leaked handlers. Node.js's built-in `onTimeout` only destroys free-pool sockets, not active ones, so the request survives.

### Empirical Verification

Minimal reproduction scripts confirm the issue:

| Scenario | Result |
|---|---|
| `node-fetch` POST alone (8s response) | **Success** at 8s |
| `http.request` GET then `node-fetch` POST (8s) | **Success** at 8s |
| **axios** GET then `node-fetch` POST (8s) | **Fails** with "socket hang up" at ~5s |

After an axios request, the socket has **2** timeout listeners (`onTimeout` + `socket.destroy`). After a raw `http.request`, only **1** (`onTimeout`).

Stack trace of the leaked listener:

```
socket.addListener(timeout): anonymous (IS socket.destroy)
  at RedirectableRequest.destroyOnTimeout (node_modules/follow-redirects/index.js:212:12)
  at RedirectableRequest.emit (node:events:519:35)
  at eventHandlers.<computed> (node_modules/follow-redirects/index.js:49:24)
```

### Suggested Fix

Use a dedicated `http.Agent({ keepAlive: false })` for all axios requests to `CODE_BASEURL` in:
- `api/server/services/Files/Code/crud.js` (`getCodeOutputDownloadStream`, `uploadCodeEnvFile`)
- `api/server/services/Files/Code/process.js` (`processCodeOutput`, `getSessionInfo`)

This prevents tainted sockets from entering the global pool where `node-fetch` could reuse them. Verified working with the same reproduction test.

```js
const http = require('http');
const codeServerAgent = new http.Agent({ keepAlive: false });

// Add to every axios call targeting CODE_BASEURL:
const response = await axios({
  // ...existing config...
  httpAgent: codeServerAgent,
});
```

### Environment

- Node.js: v23.1.0
- axios: 1.13.5
- follow-redirects: 1.15.11
- node-fetch: 2.7.0
danny-avila/LibreChat#12298.title: Code executor: socket hang up after 5s when files are attached (Node.js v23 + follow-redirects leak)

danny-avila/LibreChat#12302.body:
### What Happened?

Uploading an `.odt` file via "Upload as Text" on an agent appears to succeed with no error shown to the user, but the model receives the raw binary content of the file instead of extracted text. The model recognizes it as a ZIP/XML structure but cannot read its content.

ODT support was partially introduced in #11959, which added the MIME type to the allowlist but two pieces were missing for the full pipeline to work:
1. `documentParserMimeTypes` in `packages/data-provider/src/file-config.ts` was missing `application/vnd.oasis.opendocument.text`, so ODT was never routed through the document parser pipeline in `processAgentFileUpload`
2. `getParserForMimeType()` in `packages/api/src/files/documents/crud.ts` had no ODT handler, so even if routed correctly, `parseDocument()` would throw `Unsupported file type in document parser: application/vnd.oasis.opendocument.text`

ODS (spreadsheets) works correctly. PDF and DOCX also work correctly.


**Root cause**
`documentParserMimeTypes` in `packages/data-provider/src/file-config.ts` was missing ODT:
```typescript
export const documentParserMimeTypes = [
  excelMimeTypes,
  /^application\/pdf$/,
  /^application\/vnd\.openxmlformats-officedocument\.wordprocessingml\.document$/,
  /^application\/vnd\.oasis\.opendocument\.spreadsheet$/,
  // ❌ application/vnd.oasis.opendocument.text missing
];
```

And `getParserForMimeType()` in `packages/api/src/files/documents/crud.ts` had no ODT handler:

```typescript
// Only ODS is handled ✅
if (
  excelMimeTypes.test(mimetype) ||
  mimetype === 'application/vnd.oasis.opendocument.spreadsheet'
) {
  return excelSheetToText;
}
return undefined; // ODT falls through ❌
```

Fix
A fix has been submitted in PR #12303 
References: #11959

```
registry.librechat.ai/danny-avila/librechat-dev:latest   5ae3e6bc7d10   3.42GB   752MB

**Actual behavior**

However, within the binary data, it appears to include one or more images (Thumbnails/thumbnail.png), as well as several XML files (manifest.rdf, settings.xml, meta.xml, and content.xml), which are standard components of an .odt document."_

danny-avila/LibreChat#12306.body:
### What happened?

When deploying a new version or restarting the server, role-level permissions for agents are reset to the `interfaceSchema` defaults. This causes two problems:

1. **Users gain agent creation ability they shouldn't have.** We configure `interface.agents: { use: true }` in `librechat.yaml` to enable agents without specifying `create`, `share`, or `public` — expecting those to be preserved from the database (set via admin panel). After a restart, the USER role's `CREATE` permission reverts to `true` (the schema default), making the agent builder visible to users who should only consume pre-shared agents.

2. **Globally shared agents lose visibility.** Agents shared with all users via ACL become inaccessible after a deploy because the `SHARE` / `SHARE_PUBLIC` permissions revert to `false` (the schema default).

This was previously reported in #11522, which describes identical symptoms but was closed without a fix or linked PR.

### Root cause

`updateInterfacePermissions()` in `packages/api/src/app/permissions.ts` iterates over both the USER and ADMIN roles (line 102) and applies the **same** values derived from `librechat.yaml`. There is no mechanism to set different permission values per role.

The `interfaceSchema` default for agents is `{ use: true, create: true, share: false, public: false }` (defined in `packages/data-provider/src/config.ts`). When a permission type is first initialized or when a new field is backfilled (e.g., SHARE/SHARE_PUBLIC added in PR #11283), both roles receive these same defaults — which is too permissive for USER (create: true) and too restrictive for ADMIN (share: false).

### The underlying gap

The yaml `interface.agents` config applies uniformly to all roles. In practice, organizations need different permission sets per role — for example:

- **Admins**: can create, share, and publish agents
- **Users**: can only use pre-shared agents (no create, no share)

Today there's no way to express this in `librechat.yaml`. The only workaround is to set values via the admin panel after every deploy, which defeats the purpose of config-as-code.

### Suggested approach

One option would be to add an optional `admin` override block within `interface.agents`:

```yaml
interface:
  agents:
    use: true        # default for all roles
    create: false    # users can't create
    share: false     # users can't share
    public: false    # users can't share publicly
    admin:           # override for admin role
      create: true
      share: true
      public: true
```

Top-level values apply to all roles (effectively the user defaults). The `admin` section selectively overrides for the ADMIN role, inheriting from top-level for any omitted field. Configs without the `admin` key would work exactly as they do today.

This same pattern could extend to other permission types (prompts, MCP servers, etc.) if needed.

That said, we'd love to hear if there's an existing or planned approach we're not aware of — perhaps something in the admin settings API, the role system, or an upcoming feature that already addresses per-role permission differentiation via config.

### Version Information

v0.8.3 (Docker deployment with persistent MongoDB)

### Steps to Reproduce

1. Configure `librechat.yaml` with `interface: { agents: { use: true } }` (no `create`, `share`, or `public` specified)
2. Start the server — USER role gets `AGENTS.CREATE = true` from schema defaults
3. Set USER role `AGENTS.CREATE = false` via admin panel
4. Restart the server (or deploy a new image)
5. USER role `AGENTS.CREATE` reverts to `true` — agent builder is visible to regular users again

### Relevant log output

```shell
On startup, debug logs show:
  Role 'USER': Applying explicit config for 'AGENTS'
  Updating 'USER' role permission 'AGENTS' 'USE' from undefined to: true

The AGENTS permission type is treated as explicitly configured (because interface.agents
is set in yaml), but only USE is specified. CREATE/SHARE/SHARE_PUBLIC either get schema
defaults on first init or are backfilled with defaults on subsequent restarts when the
field is missing from the DB.
```
