danny-avila/LibreChat#12389.body:
## Bug Description

The Agents API (`/api/agents/v1/chat/completions`) fails with `no_user_key` error when the agent's provider is a custom endpoint configured with `apiKey: "user_provided"`.

## Root Cause

In `packages/api/src/endpoints/custom/initialize.ts`, the user's stored API key is only fetched when `expiresAt` (from `req.body.key`) is truthy:

```ts
const { key: expiresAt } = req.body;

let userValues = null;
if (expiresAt && (userProvidesKey || userProvidesURL)) {
  checkUserKeyExpiry(expiresAt, endpoint);
  userValues = await db.getUserKeyValues({ userId: req.user?.id ?? '', name: endpoint });
}

const apiKey = userProvidesKey ? userValues?.apiKey : CUSTOM_API_KEY;
```

In the **regular UI chat flow**, the frontend sends `req.body.key` containing the key's expiration timestamp → `expiresAt` is truthy → key is fetched → works fine.

In the **Agents API flow**, `req.body` is an OpenAI-compatible payload (`{ model, messages, stream }`) which does **not** include a `key` field → `expiresAt` is `undefined` → the key is never fetched → `userValues` stays `null` → `apiKey` is `undefined` → throws `NO_USER_KEY`.

## Steps to Reproduce

1. Configure a custom endpoint with `apiKey: "user_provided"` in `librechat.yaml`:
   ```yaml
   endpoints:
     custom:
       - name: "MyProxy"
         apiKey: "user_provided"
         baseURL: "https://my-proxy.example.com/v1"
   ```

2. Enable remote agents:
   ```yaml
   interface:
     remoteAgents:
       use: true
       create: true
   ```

3. Create an agent using the custom endpoint provider

4. Generate an Agents API key from the UI

5. Call the Agents API:
   ```bash
   curl -X POST "https://your-instance/api/agents/v1/chat/completions" \
     -H "Authorization: Bearer <AGENT_API_KEY>" \
     -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
     -d '{
       "model": "<AGENT_ID>",
       "messages": [{"role": "user", "content": "Hello"}]
     }'
   ```

6. Response: `{"error":{"message":"{\"type\":\"no_user_key\"}","type":"server_error"}}`

## Expected Behavior

The Agents API should retrieve the user's stored API key from the database regardless of whether `req.body.key` is present.

## Proposed Fix

In `packages/api/src/endpoints/custom/initialize.ts`, decouple the key fetch from the expiry check:

```ts
let userValues = null;
if (userProvidesKey || userProvidesURL) {
  if (expiresAt) {
    checkUserKeyExpiry(expiresAt, endpoint);
  }
  userValues = await db.getUserKeyValues({ userId: req.user?.id ?? '', name: endpoint });
}
```

This way:
- **UI flow**: `expiresAt` is present → checks expiry AND fetches key (same as before)
- **Agents API flow**: `expiresAt` is absent → skips expiry check, still fetches key (fixes the bug)

The expiry check is a secondary validation — if a key is truly expired, the upstream API call will fail with an auth error anyway.

## Environment

- LibreChat: latest (ghcr.io/danny-avila/librechat:latest)
- Custom endpoint with `apiKey: "user_provided"` backed by LiteLLM proxy
- Agents API enabled via `remoteAgents.use: true`
danny-avila/LibreChat#12389.title: [Bug] Agents API: no_user_key error when custom endpoint uses apiKey: user_provided

danny-avila/LibreChat#12415.body:
### What happened?

We found that MCP OAuth detection in the LLM-initiated tool-call flow depends too much on the tool-call name format.

In the tool-call UI, MCP OAuth preparation is currently decided in the client based on whether the tool name contains the MCP delimiter:
[client/src/components/Chat/Messages/Content/ToolCall.tsx]

However, in the LLM-initiated OAuth flow, the backend can emit a tool-call placeholder whose name is only the MCP server name:
[api/server/services/MCP.js]

That means the client may not recognize the flow as MCP-related, even though the OAuth URL is clearly an MCP callback such as:

/api/mcp/<serverName>/oauth/callback

## Where this happens

This issue is specifically about OAuth flows that are initiated by the LLM through a tool call, not the normal manual MCP initialization path.

## Relevant code paths:

LLM OAuth tool-call event creation:
[api/server/services/MCP.js]
Client-side MCP detection before opening OAuth:
[client/src/components/Chat/Messages/Content/ToolCall.tsx]

## Why this is fragile

The client is using the tool-call name as the main signal for whether it should treat the flow as MCP OAuth.

That is not reliable in the LLM-driven flow, because the emitted name may be just the server name instead of an MCP-formatted tool name. As a result, the client can skip MCP-specific OAuth preparation even in a valid MCP OAuth flow.

## Expected behavior

For LLM-initiated MCP OAuth flows, LibreChat should not rely only on the tool-call name format. It should use the OAuth redirect URL as a source of truth and detect MCP flows from the callback path.

### Version Information

f277b3203040020579b687f9656b45d35813d8f4

1.) configure an mcp server
2.) ask the llm to call a tool


danny-avila/LibreChat#12494.body:
### What happened?

I spent a lot of time debugging why one of the tools from the MCP-server did not work. The name was something like "get_action", since there is an entity named action in the database. The tool showed up in the GUI when creating an agent, but the models could not use it since they did not know about it.

Changing name to "fetch_action", "retrieve_action" etc did not work, but it started to work when i did not use the word action in the name. I researched it further and found that there is a variable named actionDelimiter that was defined to `_action_` and caused this issue. I did not research further exactly why this variable is used, but it seems like it used to match for tool-names and if they match they are interpreted as something else. Since the check is done after the namespace is appended it matched my tool name.

I am sure there is a good reason for this check, but if this is an internal value with special meaning, I think the value must be more unique so it doesnt match "normal" tool names. It was very hard to find since it showed up as a loaded tool in the GUI, and there was no warning/error logs.

danny-avila/LibreChat#12507.body:
### What happened?

HTML artifacts currently trigger a Sandpack runtime console error because LibreChat passes the Tailwind Play CDN URL through `externalResources`:

```ts
externalResources: ['https://cdn.tailwindcss.com/3.4.17']
```

That URL has no `.js` or `.css` suffix, and Sandpack's `static` preview path tries to infer resource type from the URL extension. As a result, HTML artifact previews log:

```text
Runtime injection failed Error: Unable to determine file type for external resource: https://cdn.tailwindcss.com/3.4.17
```

## Observed behavior

- HTML artifacts (`text/html`) render, but log the runtime injection error in the console
- React artifacts do not show the same error in the same way
- This makes the issue appear inconsistent, but the root cause is the static Sandpack path

## Root cause

LibreChat uses shared Sandpack options for all artifact previews, but only the `static` template path hits the failing external resource injection logic.

Relevant files:
- `client/src/utils/artifacts.ts`
- `client/src/components/Artifacts/ArtifactPreview.tsx`

## Suggested fix

Do not pass `externalResources` when the preview template is `static`.

Keep the Tailwind `<script>` in the shared `/public/index.html` override, but omit `externalResources` for static HTML previews only.

Example:

```ts
const options: SandpackProviderProps['options'] = useMemo(() => {
  const baseOptions = template === 'static' ? {} : sharedOptions;

  if (!startupConfig) {
    return baseOptions;
  }

  return {
    ...baseOptions,
    bundlerURL: template === 'static' ? startupConfig.staticBundlerURL : startupConfig.bundlerURL,
  };
}, [startupConfig, template]);
```

## Why not remove `externalResources` globally?

A global removal can regress React artifact styling in this setup. The safer fix is to scope the omission to static previews only.

### Version Information

f277b3203040020579b687f9656b45d35813d8f4

### Steps to Reproduce

create an artifact with the following prompt and check the console

Create a live-preview artifact, not a chat code block.

Return exactly one artifact using LibreChat artifact syntax for a complete single-file HTML page.

Requirements:
- use :::artifact with type="text/html"
- include a working HTML document
- include <script src="https://cdn.tailwindcss.com"></script> inside the HTML
- style the page with Tailwind classes
- do not explain anything
- do not use a normal markdown code block outside the artifact

### What browsers are you seeing the problem on?

Chrome

### Relevant log output

```shell
2sandpack.5xGeeRT4.js:1 Runtime injection failed Error: Unable to determine file type for external resource: https://cdn.tailwindcss.com/3.4.17
    at sandpack.5xGeeRT4.js:1:131880
    at Array.map (<anonymous>)
    at t.injectExternalResources (sandpack.5xGeeRT4.js:1:131650)
    at w.getFileContent (sandpack.5xGeeRT4.js:1:130224)
    at w.getIndexAtPath (vendor.ii3c4ZsT.js:1:2433691)
    at w.handleWorkerRequest (vendor.ii3c4ZsT.js:1:2434031)
```
